label: Celto-Germanic subnode / monophyleticity
summary: Uniquely shared Celtic and Germanic lexical commonalities could reflect a period of shared linguistic evolution or overlooked inherited archaisms/shared innovations from the PIE dialectal period.

label: Mutual contact
summary: Lexical material could have been exchanged horizontally after the breakup of Indo-European during prehistoric contact between Celtic and Germanic groups in Europe.

label: Shared contact with non-Indo-European languages
summary: Celtic and Germanic may both have borrowed vocabulary from non-Indo-European languages spoken in Europe before or during steppe-related incursions.
